cards.front: What is the primary function of the Encoder in the Transformer architecture?
cards.back: The Encoder maps an input sequence of symbol representations (x) to a sequence of continuous representations z.

cards.front: What is the role of the Decoder in the Transformer architecture?
cards.back: The Decoder generates an output sequence (y) one element at a time, taking the continuous representations z from the Encoder as input.

cards.front: How does the Encoder-Decoder architecture process sequential data?
cards.back: It utilizes stacked self-attention and point-wise, fully connected layers for both the encoder and decoder, operating on the sequence as a whole rather than sequentially.
